name: cashews (Async Cache with Built-In Strategies)

auth.notes: No auth for memory backend; Redis URL for Redis backend

performance.notes: Memory backend: microseconds; Redis backend: network latency; async-native

agent_readiness.notes: Async cache with built-in strategies for FastAPI/async Python. from cashews import cache. cache.setup('mem://'). @cache(ttl='1h', key='user:{user_id}'). async def get_user(user_id): .... Early invalidation: @cache.invalidate. Rate limit: @cache.rate_limit(10, period='1m', key='{user}'). Circuit breaker: @cache.circuit_breaker. Bloom filter: cache.bloom. Cache-aside, read-through, write-through. Redis: cache.setup('redis://localhost'). DiskCache backend. Probabilistic early expiration (XFetch). Type-safe keys via templates.

gotchas: Less mature than aiocache — fewer production deployments
Some advanced strategies (XFetch, bloom) require tuning
Documentation is less comprehensive than aiocache or cachetools
best_when: FastAPI/async Python apps needing decorator-based caching
When rate limiting and circuit breaker alongside caching is needed
Redis-backed async caching with type-safe key templates
When XFetch probabilistic expiration prevents thundering herd
avoid_when: Sync Python applications (use cachetools or diskcache)
Production critical paths where aiocache's maturity is preferred
summary: cashews provides async caching with built-in strategies for Python. @cache(ttl='1h', key='user:{user_id}'). Redis and memory backends. Rate limit: @cache.rate_limit(10, period='1m'). Circuit breaker: @cache.circuit_breaker. Bloom filter. XFetch probabilistic expiration. Type-safe key templates. MIT. Best for FastAPI apps needing decorator-based async caching with advanced strategies like rate limiting and circuit breakers.
